benefit: Catch a leaked API key before it ever runs
description: Intercepts every shell command and scans it for API keys, tokens or plaintext passwords before execution. Blocks the command if a secret pattern is found.
name: Secret detection before Bash execution
use_cases: Credential protection
DevSecOps audit
Leak prevention in CI/CD

benefit: Stops a disk-wiping shell command before it runs
description: Blocks potentially destructive shell commands before the agent runs them: wiping root, home or CWD, git reset --hard, TRUNCATE, mkfs, dd to a disk, chmod 777 recursively.
name: Destructive command blocking
use_cases: Guardrail on dev machines
Data loss prevention
Shared environments
Database safety

benefit: Your .env and keys stay untouched by the agent
description: Prevents the agent from modifying sensitive files (.env, secrets, private keys, CI lockfiles) by intercepting the Write and Edit tools and checking the target path.
name: Sensitive file write protection
use_cases: Configuration protection
Compliance
Local secrets integrity

benefit: Lock files stay intact - package manager only
description: Prevents the agent from directly modifying lock files (pnpm-lock.yaml, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Cargo.lock, Gemfile.lock, poetry.lock, etc.). Lock files must only be written by the package manager to stay consistent and reproducible.
name: Lock file write protection
use_cases: Prevent lock file corruption
Ensure reproducible builds
Multi-ecosystem projects (npm, pnpm, yarn, cargo, bundler)

benefit: No accidental push straight to main
description: Detects direct git push commands to main/master and blocks them to force going through a branch and a pull request.
name: git push to main guardrail
use_cases: Branch discipline
Main branch protection
GitFlow workflow

benefit: The agent follows your conventions, unprompted
description: On every user prompt, automatically injects the project conventions (stack, style rules, constraints) by reading a conventions file, so the agent stays aligned without manual reminders.
name: Project conventions injection
use_cases: Code consistency
Agent onboarding
Team standards

benefit: Starts every session knowing your repo state
description: At session start, injects the repository state: current branch, git status, latest commits. The agent starts with a clear view of the project state.
name: Load Git context at startup
use_cases: Context resumption
Git awareness
Multiple sessions

benefit: No more answers stuck on last year's date
description: Adds the current date and time to the context of every prompt, preventing the agent from reasoning with an incorrect cutoff date.
name: Current date and time injection
use_cases: Correct temporal reasoning
Timestamped logs
Changelogs

benefit: Every file lands already Prettier-clean
description: After every file write or edit, runs Prettier on the modified file to ensure consistent formatting without agent intervention.
name: Automatic formatting after write
use_cases: Style consistency
Reduced diff noise
Code quality

benefit: Lint errors fixed in the same loop, not in CI
description: Runs Biome on the file just written and returns the errors to the agent so it fixes them immediately within the same loop.
name: Biome lint after write
use_cases: Continuous quality
Auto-fix loop
Lint standards

benefit: Type errors caught the moment a file is saved
description: Runs the TypeScript compiler in no-emit mode after a .ts/.tsx file is modified and reports typing errors back to the agent.
name: TypeScript type checking
use_cases: Type safety
Early error detection
Strict TypeScript projects

benefit: Won't hand back until the test suite is green
description: When the agent finishes its response, runs the tests related to your pending changes (vitest --changed / jest --onlyChanged; full suite for other runners). Failures send the agent back to fix them before handing back. Skips entirely when only docs changed, so those sessions end instantly.
name: Run tests at end of response
use_cases: Assisted TDD
Regression safety net
Quality before handoff

benefit: Pings your Slack when the agent needs you
description: Relays agent notifications (permission request, awaiting input) to a Slack channel via webhook, useful for long or unattended sessions.
name: Slack notification on agent event
use_cases: Long sessions
Remote supervision
Team collaboration

benefit: Every session leaves an audit trail
description: At session end, writes an audit line (timestamp, end reason, directory) to a local log file for DevSecOps traceability.
name: End-of-session audit log
use_cases: Traceability
DevSecOps audit
Usage reporting

benefit: See exactly how hard each session works
description: Increments a counter per tool type after each use and logs the activity, providing a view of the agent's usage intensity per session.
name: Tool usage tracking
use_cases: Observability
Usage analysis
Agentic FinOps

benefit: An automatic changelog of what the agent shipped
description: At session end, captures the diff of modified files and appends it to a timestamped change log, creating an automatic changelog of what the agent produced.
name: Session summary generation
use_cases: Automatic changelog
Change traceability
Post-hoc review

benefit: Forces uv and pnpm, kills pip/npm drift
description: Blocks pip, poetry, npm and yarn commands; enforces uv (Python) and pnpm (Node.js). Generates an actionable message with the replacement command.
name: Enforce package managers
use_cases: Standardize package management tooling across a Python+Node monorepo
Avoid virtual environment conflicts between pip and uv

benefit: Edits never leak back into your main repo
description: Blocks any Edit/Write whose absolute path targets the main repo instead of the active worktree. Suggests the corrected path. Detects the worktree via .claude/active-worktree or via git rev-parse.
name: Worktree guardrail (Edit/Write)
use_cases: Prevent accidental changes to the main repo in worktree mode
Parallel multi-agent workflows with per-worktree isolation

benefit: Types and lint must pass before Claude hands back control
description: Runs a full quality gate when the session stops: TypeScript typecheck and Biome lint. Tests are intentionally left to the stop-run-tests hook to avoid running the suite twice. Blocks the stop with actionable output if any check fails.

hooks.rewakeMessage: Des erreurs qualité ont été détectées après la dernière modification :

name: Automatic quality check (Stop)
use_cases: Automatic Definition of Done with no manual intervention
Block sessions that leave lint errors or insufficient coverage
Parallelize build + tests to minimize wait time

benefit: New worktrees boot with their own env and ports
description: On session start inside a freshly created worktree, copies the main repo's .env files into the worktree so it is ready to run.

hooks.statusMessage: Copie des .env du worktree au démarrage de session...

name: Worktree env initialization
use_cases: Isolate environment variables (ports, secrets) per worktree
Start multiple agents in parallel without port collisions

benefit: Every worktree starts with fresh dependencies
description: On session start inside a worktree that has no node_modules yet, spawns the dependency install as a detached background process so the session never blocks.

hooks.statusMessage: Mise à jour des dépendances du worktree...

name: Worktree dependency update
use_cases: Ensure every worktree starts with up-to-date dependencies
Automate bootstrapping of a new isolated agent environment

benefit: No source file ships without a test
description: On every session stop, inspects git diff to list modified source files and checks that a matching test file exists. Fails with the list of uncovered files if tests are missing.
name: Missing test detection (Stop)
use_cases: Ensure no business file ships without an associated test
Integrate a lightweight 'Definition of Done' without running the full test suite

benefit: Catches the file that quietly dropped coverage
description: Reads the JSON coverage report (vitest/jest coverage-summary.json or pytest coverage.json) and checks that each file modified since the merge base reaches ≥80% line coverage. Reports files below the threshold with their exact percentage.
name: Per-modified-file coverage (Stop)
use_cases: Block a session that lowered coverage on a specific file without seeing it in global coverage
Focus feedback on the files actually modified rather than the whole project

benefit: Blocks the session on a lint error you introduced
description: Runs Biome (--error-on-warnings) on each .js/.ts/.jsx/.tsx file modified since the merge base. Reports files with lint problems and auto-disables after 3 consecutive failures so it never gets stuck blocking a session.
name: Per-modified-file lint (Stop)
use_cases: Catch a lint regression on the exact files changed in this session, not the whole project
Keep the definition-of-done green without running a full project lint

benefit: A flaky Stop hook can't trap your session
description: Per-session deduplication mechanism: uses $PPID as a stable session identifier, counts consecutive failures in /tmp, and automatically disables the hook after ≥3 failures without a fix. Shows reactivation instructions. Resets the counter on success. Prevents infinite asyncRewake re-wake loops.
name: Auto-disable Stop hook after N failures
use_cases: Prevent a flaky Stop hook from blocking a session indefinitely via asyncRewake
Automatically suspend quality checks when errors are known and being fixed
Protect any potentially unstable Stop hook without changing its core logic

benefit: Ship no untranslated string, no orphan key
description: Validates the consistency of translation files on every session stop: every key used in the code exists in the i18n files, and no orphan key lingers. Runs only if frontend files were modified.
name: Full i18n validation (Stop)
use_cases: Prevent shipping untranslated strings or orphan keys
Validate translations automatically without waiting for CI

benefit: Hear it out loud when the agent needs you
description: Announces out loud via the system text-to-speech (say/espeak) when the agent needs the user's attention.
name: TTS voice notification
use_cases: Alert the user during long autonomous runs
Audible notification when the agent awaits a validation

benefit: Get told out loud the moment work is done
description: Announces out loud via TTS that the agent has finished its task, with a random completion message.
name: Voice task-completion announcement
use_cases: Get notified without watching the screen when the task is done
Audio feedback at the end of a long session

benefit: A chime to catch your attention, no voice needed
description: Plays a system sound (Glass.aiff on macOS, paplay on Linux, PowerShell beep on Windows) when the agent needs the user's attention. A silent alternative to TTS that works offline and requires no voice engine.
name: System sound notification
use_cases: Alert the user during long autonomous runs
Works in silent environments where TTS is unwanted
Minimal-distraction attention signal

benefit: A satisfying chime the moment Claude finishes
description: Plays a completion sound (Hero.aiff on macOS, paplay on Linux, PowerShell beep on Windows) when the agent stops. Instant audio feedback without TTS, no voice engine required.
name: System sound on task completion
use_cases: Audio feedback when a long task finishes
Minimal-distraction alternative to TTS
Complement stop-tts-completion with a lighter signal

benefit: Hear each subagent spin up
description: Announces out loud via TTS when a subagent starts, to track parallel agent activity.
name: Subagent start voice announcement
use_cases: Audio traceability of parallel agents
Track subagent startup without a visual interface

benefit: Hear each subagent finish, no audio clash
description: Announces the end of a subagent with a voice message. Uses a file lock to avoid TTS overlap when several subagents finish in parallel.
name: Subagent end voice summary
use_cases: Audio tracking of completed parallel tasks
Ordered subagent-end announcements without sound collisions

benefit: Each session gets a name, not a hash
description: Automatically generates a creative one-word name for each session (e.g. Phoenix, Sage, Nova) on the first prompt, for more human-friendly session tracking.
name: Automatic session naming
use_cases: Identify sessions in logs by a memorable name
Personalize subagent TTS announcements

benefit: Your full transcript survives every compaction
description: Automatically backs up the JSONL transcript file before every context compaction, to allow auditing or restoration.
name: Transcript backup before compaction
use_cases: Keep a full history before compaction
Audit long sessions

benefit: Stop clicking approve on harmless reads
description: Automatically approves permission requests for read-only operations (Read, Glob, Grep, safe Bash commands) without manual intervention.
name: Auto-approve read-only permissions
use_cases: Reduce interruptions for non-destructive operations
Automated security policy based on command safety

benefit: Every tool failure logged for the post-mortem
description: Records every tool failure into a structured JSON file with timestamp, tool name, input and error, for debugging and auditing.
name: Structured tool failure log
use_cases: Debug tools that fail during autonomous sessions
Detect recurring failure patterns

benefit: Open a session, environment's already ready
description: Checks for the required system tools and automatically installs the project dependencies (npm ci, uv sync) at session startup.
name: Dependency check and install at startup
use_cases: Environment always ready as soon as a session opens
Automatic onboarding on a new project

benefit: Sessions end with a clean logs directory
description: Automatically removes temporary files (.tmp) and stale artifacts from the logs directory at session end.
name: Temporary file cleanup at session end
use_cases: Keep a clean logs directory without manual intervention
Free disk space at the end of a long session

benefit: Conventions survive a context compaction
description: Re-injects the project conventions and critical context into the session after Claude Code compacts the context window, preventing the loss of important information.
name: Re-inject context after compaction
use_cases: Recall the project conventions (package manager, code style) after compaction
Re-inject the current sprint or critical tasks
Recall important test or build commands

benefit: Every settings.json change is on the record
description: Records every change to a Claude Code configuration file (settings.json, skills, etc.) into a timestamped audit log for traceability and compliance.
name: Configuration change audit log
use_cases: Track who changed the Claude Code configuration and when
Compliance audit on settings.json changes
Detect unauthorized configuration changes

benefit: Bash always runs in the right env per folder
description: Automatically reloads environment variables via direnv when Claude changes its working directory, ensuring Bash commands use the right environment for the current directory.
name: Reload direnv on directory change
use_cases: Mono-repo projects with different environments per subfolder
Automatic .envrc management with direnv
Sync CLAUDE_ENV_FILE with the current directory's environment

benefit: Plans proceed without the extra approval click
description: Automatically approves the ExitPlanMode permission request, removing the interactive dialog every time Claude finishes presenting a plan and asks to proceed.
name: Automatic ExitPlanMode approval
use_cases: Planning workflows where manual approval is always granted
CI/CD automation with plan mode enabled
Reduce interruptions during intensive interactive sessions

benefit: One tsc pass after a batch, not one per file
description: Runs tsc --noEmit once after a full batch of parallel tool calls that include TypeScript file edits. Avoids redundant per-file tsc invocations and reports type errors back to Claude as context for the next turn.
name: Typecheck after parallel file edits
use_cases: Run a single TypeScript check after Claude edits multiple .ts/.tsx files in parallel
Surface type errors immediately after batch refactors or code generation
Reduce tsc invocation overhead versus per-file PostToolUse hooks

benefit: Edit .env and Bash picks it up instantly
description: Watches .env, .env.local and .envrc files and writes updated KEY=VALUE pairs to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE when any of them change on disk. Subsequent Bash commands automatically pick up the new values without restarting the session.
name: Reload .env on file change
use_cases: Reload environment variables after editing .env without restarting Claude Code
Sync .env.local overrides into Claude's Bash environment on the fly
Keep API keys and feature flags up to date during active development

benefit: Tests rerun the moment a source file changes
description: Triggers the project test suite asynchronously whenever a watched source file changes on disk. Results are injected as context for Claude on the next turn, so Claude can react to test failures without being explicitly asked.
name: Auto-run tests when source files change
use_cases: Continuously validate that external file changes don't break tests during a session
Automatically surface test regressions when hot-reloading tools modify source files
Keep Claude informed of test status after every file system change

benefit: Removing a worktree reclaims its disk and containers
description: Runs cleanup tasks when a worktree is removed: stops any Docker Compose services declared in the worktree and removes node_modules to reclaim disk space. Prevents orphaned containers and disk bloat after isolation: worktree subagent sessions.
name: Clean up artifacts on worktree removal
use_cases: Stop Docker Compose services started inside an isolated worktree
Reclaim disk space by removing node_modules after worktree cleanup
Prevent orphaned containers when parallel subagents each run their own stack

benefit: Keep a readable trail of every compaction
description: Appends the compaction summary generated by Claude Code to a markdown log file inside the project. Provides a persistent record of what was summarized across context window resets, useful for long-running sessions and post-session review.
name: Save compaction summary to log
use_cases: Keep a human-readable history of every compaction event in the project
Review what Claude summarized at context boundaries for debugging or audit
Reconstruct the timeline of a long session from compaction summaries

benefit: Get pinged when a rate limit pauses Claude
description: Fires a desktop notification via a terminal escape sequence when Claude Code hits an API rate limit. Lets you step away and come back when the quota resets without polling the terminal.
name: Desktop notification on rate limit
use_cases: Get a desktop ping when Claude Code is paused by a rate limit
Avoid missing rate-limit pauses during long autonomous sessions
Works in iTerm2, WezTerm, Windows Terminal and other OSC-9 compatible terminals

benefit: Every API error logged for the post-mortem
description: Appends every API error (rate limit, auth failure, billing, server error, etc.) to a persistent log file with timestamp, error type, details and session ID. Enables post-mortem analysis of failed sessions.
name: Log API errors to file
use_cases: Track frequency and type of API errors across sessions
Diagnose billing or authentication issues in CI/CD pipelines
Correlate API failures with specific session IDs for debugging

benefit: Every task ties back to a [TICKET-123]
description: Blocks task creation when the subject does not start with a ticket reference in the format [PREFIX-123]. Ensures every agent-created task is traceable to a project management ticket before it enters the queue.
name: Enforce task naming conventions
use_cases: Require ticket numbers on all tasks created by agent teams (e.g. [PROJ-42])
Enforce consistent task naming in multi-agent CI pipelines
Prevent orphaned tasks with no traceability to Linear, Jira or GitHub issues

benefit: No task closes on a red build
description: Blocks a task from being marked as completed if the test suite fails. The agent receives the test output as feedback and must fix the failures before it can close the task, enforcing a green-build policy for every unit of work.
name: Test suite gate before task completion
use_cases: Prevent agents from closing tasks with broken tests in multi-agent pipelines
Enforce a green-build policy without human review for every agent task
Surface test failures back to the agent as actionable feedback

benefit: Auto-attach a checklist to any slash command
description: Injects skill-specific additional context whenever a slash command expands into a prompt. A configurable map lets you attach focused instructions (security checklist, style guide, domain rules) to specific skills without editing their definition.
name: Inject context on skill invocation
use_cases: Attach a security checklist automatically when /security-review is invoked
Inject the team coding style guide when /code-review runs
Pass domain-specific constraints to any skill without modifying its source

benefit: See what auto-mode silently blocked
description: Appends every auto-mode permission denial to a log file with timestamp, tool name, input and denial reason. Provides visibility into what the auto-mode classifier is blocking, helping you tune allow/deny rules over time.
name: Log auto-mode permission denials
use_cases: Understand which tool calls auto-mode is silently blocking in autonomous sessions
Tune allow/deny rules by reviewing the denial log after a session
Audit autonomous agent actions for compliance in CI/CD environments

benefit: Fresh clone opens with node_modules ready
description: Detects the project's package manager from lock files (pnpm, yarn, npm) and runs a frozen install on first session startup. Ensures Claude Code always starts with a complete node_modules without requiring a manual install step.
name: Install project dependencies on init
use_cases: Auto-install dependencies when Claude Code opens a freshly cloned repository
Guarantee node_modules is present before any tool calls run in a new project
Unify the dev environment setup across team members via a shared hook

benefit: Secrets never show on screen during a share
description: Intercepts assistant message text as it streams to the screen and replaces patterns matching API keys, GitHub tokens and Bearer credentials with [REDACTED] placeholders. The transcript and model context retain the original text; only the rendered display is sanitized.
name: Redact secrets from displayed output
use_cases: Prevent API keys from being visible on screen during pair programming or screen shares
Sanitize assistant output that echoes back tool results containing credentials
Compliance screen recording: ensure no secrets appear in recorded sessions

benefit: Know exactly which CLAUDE.md governed a session
description: Records every CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/*.md file loaded into context to a timestamped audit log. Captures the file path, memory scope (User/Project/Local/Managed) and load reason, providing full traceability of which instructions governed each session.
name: Audit log for CLAUDE.md file loads
use_cases: Audit which CLAUDE.md files were active during a session for compliance reviews
Debug unexpected model behaviour by reviewing which instruction files were loaded
Track lazy-loaded nested CLAUDE.md files in monorepos for instruction coverage

benefit: Python lands PEP 8-clean on every save
description: After every Python file write or edit, runs ruff format via uv to apply consistent style without any agent intervention.
name: Ruff format after write
use_cases: PEP 8 compliance without thinking about it
Consistent formatting across a team
Reduced review noise on style changes

benefit: Unused imports caught before they pile up
description: Runs ruff check --fix on the modified Python file so lint violations are reported to the agent immediately and auto-fixable rules are applied inline.
name: Ruff lint after write
use_cases: Catch unused imports and undefined names before they accumulate
Auto-fix trivial violations (isort, unnecessary pass, etc.)
Enforce project ruff rules without a CI round-trip

benefit: Python type errors caught right after an edit
description: Runs pyright via uv on the modified .py file and reports type errors to the agent so they are fixed within the same loop.
name: Pyright type checking
use_cases: Strict typing in Python projects with pyright
Catch type mismatches immediately after an edit
Pair with ruff for a complete Python quality pipeline

benefit: Forces uv, ends pip/poetry drift
description: Blocks pip install, pip3 install and poetry add/install commands and redirects the agent to the uv equivalent. Prevents virtual environment fragmentation.
name: Enforce uv for Python dependencies
use_cases: Keep dependency management consistent on a uv-based project
Avoid pip/uv conflicts in the same virtualenv
Enforce team tooling conventions without having to remind the agent

benefit: Won't hand back until pytest is green
description: When the agent finishes, runs the full pytest suite via uv. If tests fail, Claude re-enters and receives the failure output as context to fix the regressions.
name: Run pytest at end of response
use_cases: Regression safety net on every agent turn
TDD loop: write code until tests go green
Catch side-effects of refactors before control returns to the user

benefit: Always start from a fresh, up-to-date main
description: At session start on main/master, silently fetches the remote and fast-forward pulls if the branch is behind. If the branch diverges, injects a warning instead of pulling. Ensures the agent always works from a fresh baseline.
name: Auto-pull on main at session start
use_cases: Always-fresh baseline
Shared branch synchronization
CI/CD pre-work sync
Prevent stale-code edits

benefit: Start on main and you're moved to a worktree
description: At session start, detects if the current branch is main/master and automatically creates a dated git worktree (work/session-YYYYMMDD). The agent is informed of the new path and instructed to work there instead of the main repo.
name: Auto-create worktree when starting on main
use_cases: Branch discipline
Isolated sessions
Accidental main commits prevention
Multi-session workflows

benefit: Warns you about that WIP stash you forgot
description: At session start, scans the git stash list and injects a warning if any stash is older than 3 days. Prevents the common workflow trap of forgetting work-in-progress left in a stash.
name: Stale stash warning at session start
use_cases: Forgotten WIP recovery
Stash hygiene
Context resumption
Long-running projects

benefit: Blocks edits on main until you branch
description: Blocks any Write or Edit tool call when the current branch is main/master and the working directory is the principal repo (not a worktree). Forces the agent to create a branch or worktree before making file changes.
name: Block file writes on main branch
use_cases: Accidental main commits prevention
Branch discipline enforcement
Pair with worktree auto-create hook
Strict repository governance

benefit: hooks sync automatically whenever the catalogue changes
description: Triggers sync-hooks.mjs automatically after any .mjs hook file or registry.json change, keeping code_snippet, .claude/settings.json and .claude/hooks/ up to date without manual intervention.
name: Registry auto-sync
use_cases: Auto-sync settings.json after editing a hook in the catalogue
Create missing .mjs scripts immediately after adding a new hook entry

benefit: prevents accidentally pushing to a merged or closed PR branch
description: Intercepts git push commands and checks via gh CLI if the current branch has a closed or merged PR. Blocks the push with a message pointing to create a new branch from main.
name: Block push on closed PR
use_cases: Prevent pushing fixes on a branch whose PR was already merged or closed
Guide the agent to always branch from an up-to-date main

benefit: Prevents stale search results by silently adding the current year
description: Intercepts every WebSearch call and appends the current year to queries that lack temporal context (no year, no 'latest', 'recent', 'current', etc.). Ensures the agent retrieves up-to-date documentation, changelogs and release notes instead of outdated results.
name: WebSearch temporal context injector
use_cases: Keep dependency version lookups current
Avoid outdated API documentation in research tasks
Ensure changelog and release note searches return latest versions

benefit: Auto-loads your AGENTS.md so every session starts with full agent context
description: Reads AGENTS.md from the project root at session start and injects it as additional context. Works seamlessly alongside CLAUDE.md — useful for projects that use the OpenAI Codex / multi-agent AGENTS.md convention to document agent roles, capabilities and coordination rules.
name: AGENTS.md context loader
use_cases: Multi-agent projects using AGENTS.md for role definitions
Teams migrating from Codex/OpenAI Codex to Claude Code
Projects with explicit agent coordination rules

benefit: Catches missing 'use client' and legacy Pages Router patterns before they silently break
description: After each file write or edit, scans JS/TS files for common Next.js App Router mistakes: Server Components using React hooks without 'use client', Pages Router data-fetching methods (getServerSideProps/getStaticProps) inside app/, and plain / tags where next/image and next/link should be used. Blocks on critical errors, warns on best-practice issues.
name: Next.js App Router quality checker
use_cases: Catch missing 'use client' in interactive components
Detect Pages Router patterns during App Router migrations
Enforce next/image and next/link usage

benefit: Read any PDF, DOCX or PPTX as clean Markdown — slash token usage on binary files
description: Intercepts Read tool calls on binary documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, ODT, RTF, XLSX, EPUB, HTML) and converts them to Markdown via pandoc or pdftotext before Claude processes them. Injects the converted content directly, eliminating the need for Claude to parse raw binary data and drastically reducing token consumption. Falls back silently if no conversion tool is available.
name: Binary file to Markdown converter
use_cases: Reading PDF documentation without wasting tokens on binary encoding
Analysing Word or PowerPoint files in agentic workflows
Reducing context window usage when processing office documents

benefit: Stop Claude from wasting tokens trying to read .exe, .pyc, .pkl or .zip files
description: Intercepts Read tool calls on binary files (executables, compiled bytecode, archives, databases, ML model files, WebAssembly) and immediately blocks them with a helpful message. Claude cannot process binary content meaningfully — this hook prevents it from consuming tokens on garbage data and suggests Bash alternatives like `file` or `ls -lh`.
name: Binary file read blocker
use_cases: Preventing token waste on .pyc/.class bytecode files
Blocking reads of ML model weights (.pkl, .pt, .onnx)
Stopping accidental reads of archives or compiled binaries

benefit: Get a schema + row count instead of dumping 10 000 lines of JSON into context
description: Intercepts Read calls on large JSON, CSV, JSONL or TSV files (> 50 KB) and replaces the raw content with a compact summary: array length + item schema for JSON, headers + first 5 rows + total row count for CSV, first 3 line schemas for JSONL. Falls back silently for small files or parse errors.
name: Large JSON/CSV compactor
use_cases: Exploring a large data export without flooding context
Understanding package-lock.json or yarn.lock structure
Quickly grasping the schema of a CSV dataset

benefit: Fetch any web page as clean Markdown — strip boilerplate HTML before it enters context
description: Intercepts WebFetch calls, fetches the URL with curl, detects HTML responses, and converts them to Markdown via pandoc (falling back to a built-in tag stripper). Injects the converted content directly, skipping the raw HTML that Claude would otherwise process. Non-HTML responses (JSON APIs, plain text) pass through unchanged. Truncates to 30 000 chars.
name: WebFetch HTML-to-Markdown converter
use_cases: Reading documentation pages without loading CSS/JS boilerplate
Extracting article content from blog posts
Reducing token usage during web research sessions

benefit: Schema errors surface in-session, not in CI
description: Validates registry/registry.json against registry.schema.json the moment the file changes, feeding validation errors back into the session instead of waiting for the CI to reject the push. Silent when the registry is valid.
name: Registry schema validation on change
use_cases: Catch a missing required field right after editing a catalogue entry
Reject unknown fields before they ever reach a commit

benefit: Catalogue drift caught before CI rejects the PR
description: Safety net for the registry sync: runs sync-hooks --check when the session stops and blocks with a resync instruction if registry.json has drifted from the .mjs files on disk (e.g. the auto-sync hook failed silently mid-session). No-op on projects without sync-hooks.mjs.
name: Registry drift check at stop
use_cases: Catch a silently failed auto-sync before pushing
Guarantee code_snippet always matches the dogfooded scripts at session end

benefit: Animation rule violations flagged as you write them
description: Enforces the project's Motion (ex-Framer Motion) rules after every component write: no 'framer-motion' imports (use 'motion/react'), no motion.* elements under LazyMotion strict (use m.*), no manual prefers-reduced-motion queries, and no raw width/height/top/left animations. Non-blocking: reports each violation with the expected fix.
name: Motion rules guard
use_cases: Stop an agent from reintroducing motion.* after a context compaction
Keep every animation on the shared spring/easing language

benefit: No more READMEs telling two different stories
description: When a README changes, injects a reminder listing the sibling READMEs (repo root + packages/*) that carry the same product promise — CLI examples, slugs and wording must stay consistent across them. Pure context injection, never blocks.
name: Docs consistency reminder
use_cases: Propagate a CLI flag rename from the npm README to the GitHub README
Keep the website pitch and both READMEs telling the same story

benefit: No API key ever lands in a file
description: Scans the content being written (Write) or inserted (Edit new_string) for API keys, GitHub tokens, private keys and password assignments, and blocks the call before the secret ever lands on disk. Closes the file-write gap left by bash-level secret detection: an agent pasting a key into source code or a config file is stopped with a pointer to environment variables instead.
name: Write-time secret detection
use_cases: Blocking an agent from hardcoding an API key into source code
Stopping .env values from being copied into committed files
Preventing private keys from being pasted into the repo

benefit: Your .env secrets never enter the model context
description: Blocks Read calls on .env files (except .env.example, .sample, .template, .dist) so secrets never enter the model context, where they could leak into logs, transcripts or generated code. The block message suggests safe alternatives: read .env.example for variable names, or grep for a key name without exposing its value. Complements write-side protection (protected paths) with read-side coverage.
name: Env file read guard
use_cases: Keeping production credentials out of session transcripts
Preventing secrets from being echoed into generated code or docs
Nudging the agent toward .env.example instead of the real file

benefit: Leftover merge conflict markers caught instantly
description: After every file write or edit, checks the file on disk for leftover git conflict markers. Requires both an opening and a closing marker line to flag, which avoids false positives on markdown setext underlines. Non-blocking: feeds a warning back to the agent so it resolves the conflict before moving on.
name: Conflict marker detector
use_cases: Catching half-resolved conflicts after an assisted merge or rebase
Preventing conflict markers from reaching CI or production
Flagging files pasted from a conflicted state

name: Front-end visual check reminder
benefit: Never ship UI you only saw in the diff
description: After editing any front-end file — styles (css/scss/less…), markup (html), or components (tsx/jsx/vue/svelte/astro) — injects a reminder to verify the change actually renders in the browser. Detects the file kind by extension and tells the agent to load it in the preview, screenshot/snapshot it, check the console, and test the affected interaction or responsive state instead of assuming the diff looks right. Universal: fires on any project, no stack required.
use_cases: Catch layout, spacing, or color regressions the diff hides
Stop the agent from marking a UI change done without looking at it
Prompt a browser preview + screenshot on every css/html/component edit

benefit: Open PRs and CI status loaded before you ask
description: At session start, injects the repository GitHub state via the gh CLI: open pull requests (up to 5) and check status for the current branch PR. Local git context tells the agent where the working tree is; this hook covers the blind spot of what is happening on GitHub. Silent no-op when gh is missing, unauthenticated, or the repo has no GitHub remote.
name: GitHub context loader
use_cases: Resuming work with awareness of PRs awaiting review
Reacting to failing CI checks without being asked to look
Avoiding duplicate work on changes already in an open PR

benefit: Warns on code duplication above threshold at session end
description: At session end, runs jscpd to detect duplicated code blocks across source and test directories. Only fires when jscpd is installed globally (npm install -g jscpd). Non-blocking — emits a warning with clone locations so you can refactor before committing. Configurable threshold (default 5%) and minimum token count (default 50) to avoid false positives on short boilerplate.
name: Code duplication guard
use_cases: Catching copy-pasted test helpers before they spread to a fifth file
Enforcing a team duplication budget without CI slowdown
Getting a summary of technical debt accumulated in a session

benefit: Every page ships indexable — title + description set
description: After editing a Next.js App Router page (src/app/**/page.tsx), verifies it exports `metadata` or `generateMetadata` with both a title and a description — the two tags Google shows in results and weighs when ranking. Non-blocking: names exactly what is missing so no page ever goes live invisible to search.
name: SEO page metadata guard
use_cases: Stop an agent from shipping a page Google cannot rank
Keep title + description coverage at 100% across every route

benefit: Stop a raw from wrecking your LCP score
description: Flags raw tags in components (src/**/*.tsx) and routes you to next/image () — automatic LCP optimization, zero layout shift (CLS), lazy-loading and modern formats (AVIF/WebP) for free. Non-blocking: a raw is a silent Core Web Vitals regression that quietly costs you ranking.
name: Next.js image guard
use_cases: Protect your Core Web Vitals when adding imagery
Enforce next/image over raw in every review

benefit: One <h1> per page — the clean outline crawlers trust
description: Ensures a single <h1> per file in components (src/**/*.tsx). Two top-level titles confuse crawlers and screen readers about what the page is really about, diluting the keyword signal you worked to earn. Non-blocking. Deep h2-h6 nesting is a render-time concern, left to a full audit.
name: Single H1 heading guard
use_cases: Catch a duplicate <h1> after a copy-paste
Keep one clear topic signal per page for SEO

benefit: WCAG violations caught on every JSX save, no plugin needed
description: Progressive enhancement accessibility guard on src/**/*.tsx|jsx. When Biome is installed: runs its native a11y lint rules (no plugin needed) scoped with --only=a11y. When Biome is absent: falls back to 4 instant static checks (alt, tabIndex, target=_blank, onClick without keyboard). One hook, best coverage available, zero mandatory dependencies.
name: JSX accessibility guard
use_cases: Catch missing alt, invalid aria-props, or keyboard traps the moment you write them
Get full ESLint WCAG coverage when jsx-a11y is installed, static checks otherwise
Keep the UI accessible for keyboard and screen-reader users without any mandatory setup

benefit: Your SEO can't regress — gated like your test suite
description: Session-end SEO gate (Stop) for Next.js projects — a test suite for search. Verifies every page.tsx carries a title + description, the structural files exist (robots, sitemap, OpenGraph image, manifest), and every literal internal <Link href="/..."> resolves to a real route, so no broken link bleeds crawl budget. Blocks the stop (exit 2) on any regression. Runtime JSON-LD and Core Web Vitals stay with a full audit.
name: SEO structure gate
use_cases: Gate SEO structure the way tests gate your code
Catch a broken internal link before it reaches production

benefit: No code change ships without a doc/implementation/ trace
description: Blocks the session from ending if source files were modified but no doc/implementation/ file was created or updated. Detects changes via git diff and untracked files; ignores tests, config, and internal .claude/ scripts. No-op on non-git projects.
name: Force implementation doc at stop
use_cases: Zero documentation debt after an agent session
Onboarding — always find a doc/implementation/<feature>.md explaining the why and how
Maintenance — never guess what the agent did from git diff alone

benefit: Prevents credit card numbers, IBANs, SSNs and emails from ever appearing on screen
description: Redacts hard-PII (credit card numbers, IBANs, Social Security Numbers) from display at every MessageDisplay event. Email addresses are intentionally excluded — too common in legitimate dev output (configs, error messages, docstrings). Patterns are chosen to never match valid code constructs, making this safe as a default-on compliance layer for PCI-DSS and GDPR-sensitive sessions.
name: PII redactor for display
use_cases: GDPR/PCI-DSS compliance: prevent customer data from appearing during debugging sessions with database exports or log files
Screen-recording safety: ensure no PII is visible when recording pair programming sessions or demos
Audit trail: redact sensitive fields in assistant output that echoes back tool results from customer tables

benefit: No broken links in Markdown docs you ship — checked automatically at session end
description: Full-repo scan of all .md and .mdx files for broken relative links (file existence check). Covers pre-existing doc debt, not just files touched in the current session. Pure Node.js — no network requests, no external dependencies. Skips node_modules, .git, .next and .claude directories. Non-blocking warning that lists every broken reference so it can be fixed before pushing.
name: Dead link checker
use_cases: Catch dead links in README and documentation generated or updated by the agent before pushing
Ensure internal anchor links (#section) still exist after refactoring headings
Prevent broken external URLs in changelogs, guides, or API docs from reaching the main branch

benefit: No broken images in your Markdown docs — checked automatically at session end
description: Full-repo scan of all .md and .mdx files for broken image references (![alt](src)). Covers relative paths and absolute paths resolved from public/ (Next.js convention). Ignores external URLs and data: URIs — no network requests. Skips node_modules, .git, .next and .claude directories. Non-blocking warning listing every broken reference so it can be fixed before pushing.
name: Dead image checker
use_cases: Catch broken image paths in README and docs after the agent moves or renames image files
Validate Next.js public/ asset references in Markdown before pushing
Prevent missing screenshots or diagrams from appearing as broken images in GitHub or documentation sites

benefit: No "curl | sh" runs unaudited code on your machine
description: Blocks the supply-chain classic — curl/wget piped straight into sh/bash, including PowerShell iwr|iex and shell substitutions like sh -c "$(curl …)". The agent must download, inspect, then run. Quoted mentions are ignored to avoid false positives.
name: Block remote script piped to shell
use_cases: Supply-chain safety
Untrusted install scripts
Hardened CI agents

benefit: Force-with-lease only — never clobber a teammate
description: Blocks bare git push --force / -f on any branch — not just main — and points the agent to --force-with-lease, which refuses to overwrite commits it has not seen. Complements the main/master push guards for shared feature branches.
name: Force-push guard (any branch)
use_cases: Shared branch safety
Team collaboration
History protection

benefit: Flags privilege escalation before it hangs your loop
description: Non-blocking advisory whenever a Bash command reaches for sudo. Privilege escalation is rarely needed in a dev loop and a non-interactive password prompt can silently stall the agent — this surfaces it so a venv/--user/container path is considered first.
name: Sudo usage warning
use_cases: Least-privilege dev
Avoid blocked sessions
Awareness

benefit: Never waste an edit on a file the build overwrites
description: Blocks writes into generated directories — node_modules, .next, dist, build, coverage, __pycache__, .venv and friends — by checking the target path. Stops the agent from "fixing" an artifact that the next build silently regenerates.
name: Block edits to generated folders
use_cases: Avoid wasted work
Source-of-truth hygiene
Cleaner diffs

benefit: Stops a 500 KB dump from bloating your repo
description: Blocks a single Write whose content exceeds ~500 KB — almost always an accidental dump, a generated blob, or a runaway paste that balloons the repo and the diff. Pure byte-length check, no file I/O, so it is effectively free.
name: Block oversized file writes
use_cases: Repo hygiene
Reviewable diffs
Catch accidental dumps

benefit: No stray console.log or print() slips into a commit
description: After each write, scans the touched file for forgotten debug traces — console.log/debugger (JS/TS), print()/breakpoint()/pdb (Python), dbg! (Rust) — and flags them. Non-blocking and test-file aware, so it nudges without interrupting the flow.
name: Leftover debug statement guard
use_cases: Clean commits
Code review hygiene
Catch debug leftovers

benefit: A new .env can never sneak into a commit unignored
description: When the agent creates a .env-style file, it checks the nearest .gitignore for a covering rule and warns if the file would be tracked — before any secret lands in it. Skips shared templates (.env.example/.sample). Non-blocking advisory, so it pairs cleanly with hard secret-write guards.
name: Env file gitignore guard
use_cases: Secret leak prevention
Onboarding safety
Compliance

benefit: The agent codes against your real versions, not guesses
description: Injects the exact installed versions from package.json (and pyproject.toml) into every prompt, so the agent stops hallucinating APIs from the wrong major version — the single most common time-sink in vibe coding. Reads one or two files, output is capped to stay token-light.
name: Inject installed dependency versions
use_cases: Accurate API usage
Fewer version-mismatch bugs
Faster iteration

benefit: Catch a wrong Node version before it bites at build time
description: At session start, compares the active Node major against the project contract (.nvmrc, then package.json engines.node) and warns on a mismatch — so install/build version-specific bugs surface up front, not 20 minutes in. Reads at most two files, no shell spawn.
name: Node version mismatch check
use_cases: Environment sanity
Avoid version-specific bugs
Smooth onboarding
